case_name: David Timpanaro v. Jenkinson's Pavilion, Inc.

summary.brief: David Timpanaro v. Jenkinson's Pavilion, Inc.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: On September 23, 2020, plaintiffs, accompanied by their minor child, C.T., and then sixty-nine-year-old father and grandfather, Anthony Timpanaro ('decedent'), traveled to defendants' beachfront property to spend the day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The family made their way onto the beach where they settled into beach chairs and decedent and his grandson began searching for seashells and chasing seagulls.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiffs admit they observed three large signs on the property: one advertising the boardwalk, beach, arcade games and rides; another which conveyed that the 'beach was closed[,] [n]o swimming'; and another stating 'no swimming when lifeguards are off-duty.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At some point, decedent joined Lia and C.T. at the water's edge. He was not wearing shoes and had the legs of his pants 'rolled up a little.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A wave came in while they were standing on wet sand and 'it was stronger than [she] thought it would have been. And [her] feet [sank] in and [she] teetered with the wave and stepped back. [Decedent] also teetered with the wave and stepped back, and then lost his balance.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Another wave engulfed decedent, prompting David to venture into the water to pick him up and the next thing she knew, decedent was out in the ocean yelling for help.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Lia called 9-1-1 and went to the boardwalk area to help direct first responders to the area where the decedent had been hit by the wave.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: When she returned with the emergency personnel, decedent was back on the beach facedown. He died after all efforts to revive him failed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The court granted summary judgment in favor of defendants and issued a
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Plaintiffs cannot establish a prima facie case of negligence and defendants were entitled to summary judgment in their favor.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Plaintiffs' expert argued defendants were 'grossly negligent in failing to close the seawall gate.'
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiffs' expert

evidence_index.text: The court soundly concluded defendants were entitled to judgment as a matter of law, based on its finding that decedent exceeded the scope of his
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Decedent was at the beach with family, standing at the water's edge when a wave knocked him off balance and he was swept into the ocean.

focused_exam.general: Decedent was found facedown on the beach after being swept into the ocean.

requestables.name: Emergency Response
requestables.description: Details of the emergency response and resuscitation efforts

reveal.result_summary: Emergency personnel attempted resuscitation on the beach but were unsuccessful.
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates the severity of the drowning incident
reveal.decision_impact: Highlights the need for immediate and effective emergency response

timeline_phases.name: Beach Visit
timeline_phases.description: Family visits the beach on a day with no lifeguards present

decision_points.clinical_context: Beachgoers at a closed beach with no lifeguards
decision_points.prompt: What safety measures should have been in place?

options.description: Ensure clear signage and barriers to prevent beach access
options.clinical_reasoning: Proper signage and barriers could prevent access to dangerous areas.
Insufficient to prevent accidents in dangerous conditions.

expected_action_court.description: Clear signage and barriers should have been in place
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for better safety measures

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Clearer signage and barriers should have been implemented
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Duty to warn and protect invitees

patient_outcome.description: Decedent died after being engulfed by a wave

key_findings: Defendants owed no duty to close the beach
Signage was adequate to convey no swimming was allowed

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide adequate safety measures
Failure to provide adequate warning signage

court_findings.finding: Defendants not liable under the Landowner's Liability Act
court_findings.legal_significance: No duty to warn or protect under the circumstances

court_findings.finding: Defendants owed no duty to close the beach
court_findings.legal_significance: Summary judgment in favor of defendants
